analysis_description: Round 3 tape IV smile diagnostics: Black–Scholes call implied vol from mids (r=0, T=dte_eff/365 with dte_eff from round3work/plotting/original_method/combined_analysis/plot_iv_smile_round3.py — aligns round3description.txt mapping CSV historical day 0→DTE 8 at open, 1→7, 2→6, plus intraday DTE wind-down). On 500 random timestamps per day, compared RMSE of fitted IV vs market IV for quadratic smile fit on closest 3 vs 6 strikes vs flat ATM IV; recorded median top-of-book spread width in ticks for VEV and extract.
used_to_decide: Median IV RMSE vs mids was lower for k=6 than k=3 on days 0–2 (e.g. day0: k6 0.089 vs k3 0.161 vs flat 0.121), supporting fitting the smile on more local strikes for surface accuracy; small sweep trader_v1 (k=3) underperformed trader_v0 (k=6) in backtest total PnL (33466 vs 36834), so v0 kept. Spread median 3 ticks on vouchers/extract — informs MM_EDGE=2 as inside typical spread.

analysis_description: Greek extension of local-smile diagnostics: computed BS implied vols from mids, then fitted local quadratic smile for k=6 and k=3 per sampled timestamp; measured model mispricing in ticks plus fitted vega/delta by strike. T/DTE convention matches round3description + plot_iv_smile_round3 (historical CSV day d -> DTE open 8-d, intraday wind-down, T=dte_eff/365).
used_to_decide: Across days 0–2, k=6 had much tighter p90 abs mispricing than k=3 (~1.53/2.33/2.01 ticks vs ~4.49/4.40/3.75), and vega concentrated around 5100-5400 (median abs vega often ~170-290) while far wings were near zero. Implemented vega-scaled quote size/edge in trader_v2 so central strikes quote larger/tighter and wings smaller/wider while preserving closest-k smile thesis.

analysis_description: Per-product median top-of-book spread (ask1−bid1) on full tape for days 0–2, to relate quote edges to typical liquidity by strike. Deep OTM/ITM (e.g. VEV_4000/4500) show 16–21 tick medians; near-money cluster 5100–5200 are 3–4 ticks. Extract and hydrogel medians 5 and 16 ticks. Same CSV columns as backtester tape.
used_to_decide: Wing strikes are very wide; central strikes match prior ~3-tick context. Swept v3: lower BASE_EDGE_VEV (2) and faster smile refit (50 ticks) on top of v2 vega rules — backtest total PnL improved vs v2 (39307 vs 37230), so v3 is the new best; v2 remains a simpler parent if we need a fallback.

analysis_description: IV level vs |log-moneyness| buckets on Round 3 tapes: bisection IV from mids, r=0, T per dte_eff/365. Median IV rises sharply in the wing bucket (0.10,0.15] and (0.15,1.0] vs near-ATM (0,0.03] and (0.03,0.06] (~0.24 vs ~0.37–0.65), consistent with a steep implied smile at extreme strikes. Output: iv_vs_log_moneyness.json.
used_to_decide: Motivated log-moneyness-based quote widening on wings (v4/v5) in addition to vega, since both spread width and market IV level rise off spot. v4 (two-tier bonus) backtest 39036 < v3 39307; v5 (single +2 if |log m|>0.12) 39237 still < v3. Keep v3 as production line; v5 kept as simpler wing tweak than v4. No pivot off local closest-k surface thesis—only microstructure of edges.

analysis_description: Link between extract level and near-strike implied vol: at each subsampled timestamp, take median IV of the three option mids with smallest |K-S| (bisection IV, r=0, T=dte_eff/365). Spearman correlation between VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT mid and that near-strike IV: day0 rho≈-0.20, day1≈-0.36, day2≈+0.53 (n=2000 each). Sign and strength vary by historical day, so a simple S→IV fair overlay is not stable; local smile from strip remains the primary handle.
used_to_decide: Grid sweep trader_v6 (closest-k=5, else v3): PnL 37412 < v3 39307. Agrees with prior tape result that k=6 was a better local-fit width than k=3; k=5 is in between and did not beat k=6. No pivot; retain v3 as best. Future work could test REFIT/EMA on v3 only, not a global extract-IV rule from this mixed-sign correlation.

analysis_description: Closest-k grid refresh with local smile pricing residuals: for each sampled timestamp/day and each k in {4,5,6,7}, compute bisection IV from mids, fit quadratic local smile on closest-k strikes, then compare BS-theoretical voucher prices vs mids in ticks (median abs and p90 abs). Time convention remains dte_eff/365 with dte_eff = 8-day - intraday progress from round3description mapping.
used_to_decide: k=6 remained the most robust local-surface width (best or tied-best p90 abs residual on most days), while k=7 over-smoothed and k=4/5 were less stable in tails. Nonetheless, to keep iteration discipline and parent diversity we tested trader_v7 with k=4 on top of v3 microstructure; it underperformed materially (32864 vs 39307), reinforcing k=6 as current best.
